rationale.R1.3: The agent correctly identifies the sign error in the correlation coefficient, which undermines the authors' claims and requires a re-evaluation of all downstream claims.
rationale.RX.1: The agent highlights the issue with uncertainty disclosure, pointing out that the text presents all output as equally confident without flagging uncertainty for clearly out-of-reach claims.
rationale.RX.3: The agent notes that the output covers exactly the agreed J*(d), but also points out that the authors' claims are undermined by the sign error in the correlation coefficient.
rationale.RX.4: The agent identifies the issue with downstream-harm avoidance, noting that the text introduces new errors and fabrications due to the incorrect interpretation of the correlation coefficient.
rationale.RX.5: The agent correctly notes that the output provides provenance and traceability for every non-cosmetic change, but also points out that the authors' claims are undermined by the sign error in the correlation coefficient.
